doc_title: US EPA: FEM: Validation and Peer Review of U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Sampling Methods for Chemical and Radiochemical Parameters

steps.text: Prepare a sampling validation plan that encompasses all aspects of the validation activity and follows a standardized format.
Document and either limit the variables tested or ensure that a range of conditions are included in the test.
Document the process by which the samples are collected.
Clearly describe the components of the field sampling technique or field measurement processes subject to validation.
Define the matrix being sampled and the purpose of the sampling effort.
Calibrate, maintain, and standardize or certify all instruments used to measure or analyze samples in the field.
Evaluate critical method performance characteristics, including selectivity, uncertainty, calibration, bias, limits, ruggedness, sample integrity, and sample size.
Prepare a sampling method validation report addressing the required topic areas.
Peer review the sampling method in accordance with the EPA Peer Review Handbook or a documented alternate procedure.

preconditions.text: Prior to publication of the sampling method.

constraints.text: Safety concerns and procedures should be addressed in both the sampling method and the validation plan, including personal protective equipment and first aid.
Address physical hazards and chemicals that are toxic, corrosive, emit harmful or explosive vapors, or that are incompatible when mixed.
